system_prompt:
You are an expert in OpenFOAM simulation and numerical modeling. Your task is to modify and rewrite OpenFOAM files to fix the reported error. Please do not propose solutions that require modifying any parameters declared in the user requirement, try other approaches instead. You will receive a rewrite_plan. Follow it strictly: only modify files listed in rewrite_plan.target_files and apply only the requested changes. Do not modify files outside the plan. Return the complete, corrected file contents in JSON format: list of foamfile: [{file_name: 'file_name', folder_name: 'folder_name', content: 'content'}]. Ensure your response includes only modified file content with no extra text, as it will be parsed using Pydantic.
Return ONLY valid JSON (no markdown/backticks). Schema: {"list_foamfile": [{"file_name": string, "folder_name": string, "content": string}]}.
user_prompt:
<foamfiles>list_foamfile=[FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "system";
    object      controlDict;
}

application     rhoCentralFoam;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         4;

deltaT          0.002;

writeControl    runTime;

writeInterval   0.1;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable true;

adjustTimeStep  no;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

fluxScheme      Kurganov;

ddtSchemes
{
    default         Euler;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(tauMC)      Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
    reconstruct(rho) vanLeer;
    reconstruct(U)  vanLeerV;
    reconstruct(T)  vanLeer;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    "(rho|rhoU|rhoE)"
    {
        solver          diagonal;
    }

    U
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
        nSweeps         2;
        tolerance       1e-09;
        relTol          0.01;
    }

    e
    {
        $U;
        tolerance       1e-09;
        relTol          0.01;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='momentumTransport', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;

thermoType

mixture

        molWeight   28.96;
    }
    thermodynamics
    {
        Cp          1004.5;
        Hf          0;
    }
    transport
    {
        mu          0;
        Pr          1;

boundaryField
{

boundaryField

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='blockMeshDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile

// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

scale   1;

vertices
(
    // Block 0 - bottom left (x=-1 to -0.2, y=-1 to -0.2)

    // Block 3 - top right (x=-0.2 to 3, y=-0.2 to 1)
    (-0.2 0.2 -0.05) // 24
    (3 0.2 -0.05)    // 25
    (3 1 -0.05)      // 26
    (-0.2 1 -0.05)   // 27
    (-0.2 0.2 0.05)  // 28
    (3 0.2 0.05)     // 29
    (3 1 0.05)       // 30
    (-0.2 1 0.05)    // 31
);

blocks
(
    // Block 0: x=-1..-0.2, y=-1..-0.2
    hex (0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7) (20 20 1) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
    // Block 1: x=-0.2..3, y=-1..-0.2
    hex (8 9 10 11 12 13 14 15) (80 20 1) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
    // Block 2: x=-1..-0.2, y=-0.2..1
    hex (16 17 18 19 20 21 22 23) (20 20 1) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
    // Block 3: x=-0.2..3, y=-0.2..1
    hex (24 25 26 27 28 29 30 31) (80 20 1) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
);

edges
(
);

boundary
(
    inlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (0 4 7 3)
            (16 20 23 19)
        );
    }
    outlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (9 10 14 13)
            (25 26 30 29)
        );
    }
    bottom
    {
        type symmetryPlane;
        faces
        (
            (0 1 8 4)
            (16 17 21 20)
        );
    }
    top
    {
        type symmetryPlane;
        faces
        (
            (19 23 22 18)
            (27 31 30 26)
        );
    }
    obstacle
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (1 2 6 5)
            (17 18 22 21)
            (8 11 15 12)
            (24 27 31 28)
        );
    }
    defaultFaces
    {
        type empty;
        faces
        (
            (0 3 2 1)
            (4 5 6 7)
            (8 11 10 9)
            (12 13 14 15)
            (16 19 23 20)
            (20 23 26 25)
            (16 17 21 22)
            (21 22 30 29)
            (24 25 29 28)
            (27 31 26 24)
        );
    }
);

mergePatchPairs
(
);

<reviewer_analysis># Error Analysis and Resolution

## Error 1: `log.blockMesh` — Face does not have neighbour cell

**Error Message:**
```
face 0 in patch 2 does not have neighbour cell face: 4(0 1 8 4)
```

**Diagnosis:**

The error persists after two attempts, which means the blockMeshDict vertex/block/topology design is fundamentally flawed. Let me carefully trace through the current blockMeshDict.

Looking at the vertices and the problematic face `(0 1 8 4)` from the `bottom` boundary patch:

- Vertex 0: `(-1 -1 -0.05)`
- Vertex 1: `(-0.2 -1 -0.05)`
- Vertex 8: `(-0.2 -1 -0.05)` ← same as vertex 1!
- Vertex 4: `(-1 -1 0.05)`

So face `(0 1 8 4)` becomes `(0 1 1 4)` — vertex 1 and 8 are the same point, making this a triangle/degenerate face. Worse, this face is supposed to belong to the `bottom` boundary patch, but it doesn't correspond to any actual cell face in the mesh topology.

The core problem is that **Block 0 and Block 1 share the face at x=-0.2**, but the vertex numbering doesn't align properly. Specifically:

- Block 0's right face (x=-0.2) uses vertices 1, 2, 6, 5
- Block 1's left face (x=-0.2) uses vertices 8, 11, 15, 12

These are completely different vertex indices, so the shared face isn't recognized as a valid internal face. Instead, the boundary patch tries to assign face `(0 1 8 4)` — which mixes vertices from different blocks incorrectly.

**New Approach — Restructure the entire blockMeshDict:**

Instead of trying to patch the existing design, restructure the mesh to have a simpler, unambiguous topology. Here’s a recommended approach:

### Redesign Strategy

Split the domain into 3 blocks instead of 4, removing the top-left block entirely and extending Block 1 upward. This avoids the complex 4-block junction where three blocks meet at a single edge (which is extremely difficult to get right):

1. **Block A**: x=-1 to -0.2, y=-1 to -0.2 (lower-left)
2. **Block B**: x=-0.2 to 3, y=-1 to 1 (everything below the step and above, i.e., the full channel except the lower-left corner)

With 3 blocks:
- Block 0: Lower-left (x=-1..-0.2, y=-1..-0.2)
- Block 1: Upper-left (x=-1..-0.2, y=-0.2..1)
- Block 2: Right-side (x=-0.2..3, y=-1..1)

Now the shared faces between blocks use matching vertex indices:
- Block 0 and Block 1 share the face at y=-0.2, x=-1..-0.2 → vertices must be indexed identically in both blocks
- Block 0 and Block 2 share the face at x=-0.2, y=-1..-0.2 → vertices must be indexed identically

### Key Principle

For any two adjacent blocks sharing a face, the **four vertices of that shared face must be numbered identically** in both block definitions. This means if Block 0 says its right-face vertices are `(a b c d)`, then Block 1's left-face vertices must be `(b' c' d' a')` where `a=b'`, `b=c'`, `c=d'`, `d=a'` — i.e., the vertex indices themselves must be the same numbers, not just geometrically equivalent coordinates.

If restructuring to 3 blocks isn't sufficient, reduce to **2 blocks**:
- Block 0: Entire lower section (x=-1..3, y=-1..-0.2) — a wide rectangle
- Block 1: Upper section (x=-1..3, y=-0.2..1) — another wide rectangle

This eliminates the step geometry entirely from the mesh topology challenge. The step would then be modeled purely through boundary conditions rather than a geometric cutout in the mesh — but this changes the physics.

Alternatively, keep the 4-block approach but ensure **every pair of adjacent blocks shares faces with identical vertex indices**. This requires meticulous attention to vertex numbering. The critical shared faces are:
- Block 0 ↔ Block 1 at x=-0.2 (y=-1..-0.2): Block 0 uses (1,2,6,5), Block 1 must use the same indices (1,2,6,5) for that face
- Block 0 ↔ Block 2 at y=-0.2 (x=-1..-0.2): Block 0 uses (3,2,7,6), Block 2 must use the same indices
- Block 1 ↔ Block 3 at y=-0.2 (x=-0.2..3): Block 1 uses (11,10,14,12), Block 3 must use the same indices

The current blockMeshDict has each block using different vertex indices for shared faces — this is the root cause. Rewrite the blockMeshDict so that shared faces use matching vertex indices across blocks.

## Error 2: `log.rhoCentralFoam` — Cannot find "points"

**Diagnosis:** Purely cascading from blockMesh failure. No mesh exists.

**Resolution:** Fix blockMeshDict first.

## Priority Recommendation

The **single most important change** is to restructure the blockMeshDict so that adjacent blocks share faces with **identical vertex indices**. Either:
1. Use a 3-block or 2-block simpler topology, OR
2. Carefully rewrite the 4-block design ensuring that whenever Block X and Block Y share a face, the vertex indices defining that face are numerically identical in both blocks' hex definitions.

Study the official OpenFOAM forwardStep tutorial blockMeshDict very carefully — the vertex numbering convention is the key. Pay special attention to the winding order and ensure consistency at every shared interface.</reviewer_analysis>
<rewrite_plan>{'target_files': [{'file': 'system/blockMeshDict', 'changes': 'Restructure to 3 blocks to avoid complex multi-way junctions: Block 0 = lower-left region (x=-1..-0.2, y=-1..-0.2), Block 1 = upper-left region (x=-1..-0.2, y=-0.2..1), Block 2 = right-side region (x=-0.2..3, y=-1..1). Ensure shared faces between adjacent blocks use identical vertex indices. Specifically: Block 0 right face at x=-0.2 (y=-1..-0.2) must match Block 1 left face using vertices 1,2,6,5; Block 0 top face at y=-0.2 (x=-1..-0.2) must match Block 1 bottom face using vertices 3,2,7,6; Block 1 right face at x=-0.2 (y=-0.2..1) must match Block 2 left face using vertices 17,18,22,21; Block 2 bottom face at y=-0.2 (x=-0.2..3) must match nothing directly but its top face connects to Block 1. Redefine all 8 vertices carefully so that when two blocks share a face, the vertex indices are exactly the same in both hex definitions. Keep the step geometry intact.'}, {'file': 'system/fvSchemes', 'changes': 'Change fluxScheme from Kurganov to Lax; Change reconstruct rho scheme from vanLeer to vanAlbada; Change reconstruct U scheme from vanLeerV to vanLeer; Change reconstruct T scheme from vanLeer to vanAlbada'}, {'file': 'system/fvSolution', 'changes': 'Reduce U solver tolerance from 1e-09 to 1e-06; Reduce U relative tolerance from 0.01 to 0.1; Remove the e solver entry since it inherits from U via $U and add a separate entry for rho if needed; Actually keep e as-is since it inherits correctly'}]}</rewrite_plan>

<user_requirement>do a laminar, compressible flow over a forward-facing step using the rhoCentralFoam solver. Boundary conditions include a fixed velocity of 3m/s and temperature of 1 K at the inlet and slip conditions on the obstacle. Use timestep of 0.002 and output every 0.1. Finaltime is 4.</user_requirement>

Please update OpenFOAM files according to rewrite_plan only. Only include files from rewrite_plan.target_files in your output.
